heading: Pyth-2 page-110 holdouts reduced to the single e-d convention conflict
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, page110, validated-workset, convention-cleanup]
body: Re-read the local Pyth-2 witness around the page-110 leftovers and found enough in-book term definitions to promote two previously flagged periodicity items. The key source anchors were the classification block defining the par classes (`4-par` = divisible by 4, `3-par` = deficiency of one from a multiple of 4) and the tri/pent block confirming the local tri-number and pent-number divisions. With those definitions in hand, `pyth2_p110_l7747` (`3-tri` -> every fourth Fibonacci term) and `pyth2_p110_l7751` (`4-par` -> every sixth Fibonacci term) were promoted into the stable Pyth-2 lane. Rebuilt the downstream artifacts: `pyth2_validated_workset.json` now has 11 stable items with counts `6 direct_validated / 5 corrected_source_validated`, and `pyth2_validated_tables.json` now has 18 Fibonacci periodicity rows covering divisors 2, 3, 4, and 5. Only one page-110 holdout remains in `pyth2_page110_issue_register.json`: `pyth2_p110_l7765`, because the nearby e-d table convention says the lower-left `(1,1)` cell is left blank, which pushes the count to 17 and leaves the source answer `18` as a genuine local convention conflict.
